title: fix(scripts): use millisecond timestamps in detect-abuse script
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Millisecond precision
gist.blurb: Fixed a tiny time-travel bug in our internal moderation scripts. Timestamps are now correctly speaking in milliseconds instead of seconds.
gist.summary: Fixed a timestamp conversion bug in the internal abuse detection script to correctly use milliseconds when querying the database.
gist.impact: Internal moderation tools now correctly filter users by date range instead of accidentally matching the entire database.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a detective (referencing 'detect-abuse'), inspecting a glowing digital clock or hourglass to represent the timestamp fix.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny detective hat, inspecting a glowing digital clock that displays milliseconds. A chunky retro computer monitor in the background shows a green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny detective hat, inspecting a glowing digital clock that displays milliseconds. A chunky retro computer monitor in the background shows a green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
